title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A psychological thriller about a student who commits a murder and wrestles with the moral consequences. One of the greatest novels ever written.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
description: On their fifth wedding anniversary, Nick Dunne's wife Amy disappears. Under pressure from the police and the media, Nick's lies begin to unravel.

title: The Pillars of the Earth
author: Ken Follett
description: Set in 12th century England, this epic tale follows the building of a cathedral and the lives of the people whose fate becomes entwined with it.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
description: A famous painter shoots her husband five times and then never speaks again. Her therapist becomes obsessed with uncovering her motive.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: The timeless story of Elizabeth Bennet and the proud Mr. Darcy — a masterpiece of wit, romance, and social commentary.

title: All the Light We Cannot See
author: Anthony Doerr
description: The lives of a blind French girl and a German boy intersect in occupied France during World War II. Pulitzer Prize winner.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: Set in the distant future, Paul Atreides must travel to the most dangerous planet in the universe to ensure the future of his family and people.

title: And Then There Were None
author: Agatha Christie
description: Ten strangers are lured to an isolated island. One by one they are murdered. The world's best-selling mystery novel of all time.

title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
description: The autobiography of Kvothe — student, musician, and notorious wizard — told in his own words. A breathtaking work of imagination.

author: George Orwell
description: Winston Smith works for the Ministry of Truth in Oceania. He begins to question the totalitarian world he lives in — a world that punishes independent thought.

title: Wolf Hall
author: Hilary Mantel
description: The story of Thomas Cromwell, the blacksmith's son who rose to become Henry VIII's closest advisor. A Booker Prize-winning masterpiece.

title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
author: Stieg Larsson
description: Journalist Mikael Blomkvist and hacker Lisbeth Salander investigate a decades-old disappearance involving a powerful Swedish family.

title: Mistborn: The Final Empire
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: For a thousand years, ash has fallen and no flowers have bloomed. A group of rebels plan the impossible — to overthrow an immortal god-emperor.

title: In the Woods
author: Tana French
description: When a child's body is found in an Irish woodland, detective Rob Ryan leads the investigation — haunted by his own mysterious past in those same woods.
